news.summary: Trustd, a digital identity platform designed for the worldwide supply chain, has recently obtained certification under the UK Digital Identity and Attributes Trust Framework. This certification signifies that Trustd meets the necessary standards set forth in the framework to ensure secure and reliable digital identities. The platform's compliance with this framework is crucial for enhancing trust and efficiency within the supply chain industry. This achievement underscores Trustd's commitment to upholding the highest standards in digital identity verification.
news.headline: Trustd is first certified digital ID platform for transport & logistics under UK DIATF, a trust framework.

news.summary: This morning, U.S. stocks experienced a decline, with the Dow Jones index dropping over 800 points, a 2.06% decrease, reaching 39,773.54. The NASDAQ also fell by 2.93% to 16,623.63, and the S&P 500 dropped 2.51% to 5,320.16. Health care stocks are highlighted as potential gainers in Q2. In sector performance, consumer staples shares rose by 0.9%, while energy shares fell by 3.6%. The U.S. annual inflation rate decreased to 2.4% in March, from 2.8% the previous month, falling below the anticipated 2.6%. Sharps Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: STSS) saw a significant increase of 131%, with its shares valued at $0.0325. For more details, the full report is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dow plunges over 800 points in response to US inflation rate falling to 2.4% in March, lowest since Sept. 2021.

news.summary: In Q1 of 2025, the largest publicly traded Bitcoin mining companies produced nearly $800 million worth of Bitcoin. The top Bitcoin mining companies produced a total of over 9,700 Bitcoin in the first quarter, with Bitcoin trading around $81,600. Marathon Digital led with 2,285 Bitcoin mined, valued at roughly $186 million. CleanSpark followed with 1,950 BTC valued near $160 million, while Iren produced 1,513 BTC worth almost $124 million. Riot Blockchain reported production of 1,428 BTC. Hut8 Mining had the highest growth rate, mining 199 BTC in Q1 valued at $16 million, marking a 91% increase from February. Hut8 partnered with US President Donald Trump's sons to launch a new mining venture called American Bitcoin. Hut8 aims to dominate US Bitcoin mining with plans to build one of the largest and efficient Bitcoin mining platforms in the US.
news.headline: Top Bitcoin miners generated almost $800M worth of BTC in Q1 2025, mining over 9,700 BTC. Marathon Digital led with 2,285 BTC produced.

news.summary: Following the announcement of a tariff pause, the Crypto Fear & Greed Index shifted from "Extreme Fear" to "Fear." It had predominantly remained at "Extreme Fear" over the past month.
news.headline: Ethereum price rises after tariff reversal, plus strong inflation report. Fear & Greed Index shifts to fear from extreme fear during past month.

news.summary: During Distracted Driving Awareness Month in Bryan, Texas on April 10, 2025, Daniel Stark Injury Lawyers collaborated with Allie's Way to promote awareness about the dangers of distracted driving. This initiative aims to advocate for safer roads and emphasize the importance of staying focused while driving. Both organizations are dedicated to spreading this message to reduce the staggering number of accidents caused by distracted driving.
news.headline: Daniel Stark Injury Lawyers and Allie's Way team up to raise awareness on dangers of distracted driving during Distracted Driving Awareness Month in Bryan, Texas on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, in Washington, Chris Matthews' remarks on MSNBC's Morning Joe about the inability of the United States to produce additional lumber to counteract unfairly traded imports highlight media prejudice against President Trump's efforts to enforce U.S. trade regulations. The statement sheds light on the challenges faced by the U.S. in addressing trade imbalances and suggests a biased portrayal in the media against the administration's trade policy initiatives.
news.headline: U.S. Lumber Coalition criticizes Chris Matthews' remarks on MSNBC's Morning Joe over softwood lumber imports, citing media bias against President Trump's trade law enforcement.

news.summary: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who incurred losses in Alarum to reach out to him directly to explore their potential courses of action. Individuals who bought or obtained securities in Alarum within the period of March 14, 2024, to August 26, 2024, are encouraged to contact him for further discussions on their options.
news.headline: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates claims for Alarum investors between March 14, 2024 and August 26, 2024. Contact for losses.

news.summary: In Mexico City, TIENDAS 3B, a prominent grocery hard discounter, reported its consolidated financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024 and full year 2024. The company, listed on NYSE as TBBB, issued a corrected version of its earnings release on April 9, 2025. The revisions were made to the "Building Lease Payments" data for both 4Q24 and FY2024, including adjustments to the corresponding footnotes. TIENDAS 3B is known for its presence in the Mexican market and plays a significant role in the retail sector.
news.headline: Tiendas 3B releases corrected financials for 4Q24 and FY2024, reports as leading grocery hard discounter in Mexico.
BoE Breeden: The postponement of long-dated BoE gilt auction was a technical decision, not a signal of monetary policy stance.

news.summary: Nasdaq has filed an application with the SEC to list and trade shares of the VanEck Avalanche Trust - an ETF providing indirect exposure to AVAX token. The ETF, if approved, will allow investors to gain AVAX price exposure without holding the tokens. VanEck Digital Assets will sponsor the trust, and a custodian will hold the AVAX tokens. This is VanEck's fourth standalone crypto ETF filing, following BTC, ETH, and SOL products. Grayscale Investments is also pursuing an AVAX-backed ETF. Despite interest, AVAX token saw 56% losses trading at $18 on April 10 from a high of $41 in January amid the broader crypto downturn.
news.headline: Nasdaq files to list VanEck's Avalanche ETF for AVAX token, allowing indirect exposure without holding them. Source reveals known details.

news.summary: Cosmos, a blockchain network known as the "internet of blockchains," has introduced Eureka, an interoperability layer connecting its inter-blockchain communication (IBC) protocol with Ethereum. Eureka expands the Cosmos IBC protocol, transitioning it into a universal interoperability protocol for multichain apps, integrating various Cosmos projects. This development now allows Cosmos-based applications and blockchains to be accessible to more users by linking with the Ethereum network. The introduction of Eureka aims to eliminate the need for bridges and intermediaries, providing native interoperability for developers. Eureka operates through a distribution zone, offering access to IBC connections, users, liquidity, and services without extra infrastructure. Early use cases include Babylon and Elys protocols enabling cross-chain transfers of assets like Bitcoin and Wrapped tokens. Future plans involve integrating Eureka features into major decentralized exchanges and real-world asset tokenization platforms. The IBC protocol, whose upgrade is showcased through Eureka, handles up to $3 billion in monthly transaction volume across 115+ blockchains.
news.headline: Cosmos' Eureka links Ethereum and IBC networks, transforming IBC into a universal protocol. Integration with Ethereum promises expanded access to Cosmos-based apps. $3B monthly transaction volume facilitated with IBC.

news.summary: Elon Musk, renowned for his tech innovations at Tesla and SpaceX, faces backlash for his political associations, corporate choices, and public remarks. Musk’s net worth peaked at $400 billion in 2024, but declined to $298 billion by April 2025, sparking protests over wealth disparity and political influence. Criticisms include labor practices at Tesla, leading to a “Tesla Takedown” movement with 250 global protests. Tesla’s declining sales in early 2025 (336,681 units, a 13% drop year-over-year) compounded concerns. SpaceX is under scrutiny for the 2025 Starship explosion, raising safety and regulatory issues. Musk’s advisory role to Trump and DOGE leadership have contributed to his unpopularity, with polls showing 52% disapproval. Reports suggest Musk may step back from politics to salvage Tesla’s image, but the impact remains uncertain given his unconventional approach.
news.headline: Protests against Elon Musk in 2025 highlight concerns over wealth, politics, and corporate practices. Net worth dropped from $400B to $298B due to controversies. Tesla faced labor rights violation allegations and environmental criticisms, leading to a decline in global vehicle deliveries by 13% in Q1 2025. SpaceX's safety and regulatory compliance post-Starship explosion also raised issues. Despite public disapproval, Musk may step back from political involvement to focus on his businesses.

news.summary: Yesterday, it was reported that the global count of confirmed Covid-19 cases has surpassed 20 million, with more than 700,000 deaths worldwide. In the United States alone, there have been over 5 million cases and approximately 163,000 deaths. The virus continues to spread rapidly in various regions, leading to an increase in cases and deaths. Health officials emphasize the importance of following safety measures such as wearing masks, social distancing, and frequent hand washing to prevent further escalation of the pandemic. Vaccine development efforts are ongoing, with hopes for a viable solution in the near future. The situation remains critical, and it is crucial for individuals and communities to remain vigilant and proactive in combatting the virus.
news.headline: Market futures drop as CPI report fails to ease economic concerns.

news.summary: In Santa Ana, California on April 10, 2025, it was reported that nearly half (44%) of significant employers are contemplating a transition to Individual Coverage Health Reimbursement Arrangements (ICHRAs) in the next year as a strategy to control healthcare expenses. The data was part of SureCo's "2025 State of ICHRA Report," indicating a substantial shift in employer healthcare coverage trends towards ICHRAs.
news.headline: 44% of large employers considering ICHRA amid economic uncertainty from Trump tariffs; SureCo report reveals.
BoE Breeden: The recent bond market problems were in the US market in the first instance, with spillover to the UK.

news.summary: In Fairfax, Virginia and Palo Alto, California on April 10, 2025, QBurst revealed the selection of Arun Kumar Ramchandran as the new CEO. Multiples Alternate Asset Management (Multiples) took over the majority share of QBurst through a significant investment.
news.headline: QBurst appoints Arun Kumar Ramchandran ('Rak') as CEO after Multiple's investment in controlling stake.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, in London, Alok, a renowned Brazilian music artist, is scheduled to headline the INFINITY Lisbon at SBC Summit event. Known for blending Brazilian rhythms with electronic dance music, Alok will showcase his unique style to the worldwide audience. This performance is expected to draw significant attention, with an attendance estimate exceeding [insert number] people.
news.headline: Global EDM artist Alok to lead INFINITY Lisbon at SBC Summit, showcasing Brazilian fusion music, expected to draw a large audience.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) revealed a new announcement regarding recalls in partnership with various companies. The recalled products, including Fisher-Price's Brunch & Go Stroller Toys, have been identified for posing a risk of choking. Interested individuals can access further details on these recalls at www.cpsc.gov. Stay informed to ensure the safety of consumers.
news.headline: CPSC alerts on recalls including Fisher-Price Brunch & Go Stroller Toys for choking hazard on website.

news.summary: Knightscope, Inc. (NASDAQ: KSCP) has secured a lease for its new corporate headquarters in Sunnyvale, California. The 33,355-square-foot space, located at 305 North Mathilda Avenue, marks a significant milestone as the company celebrates its 12th anniversary since establishment in 2013. This move signals Knightscope's commitment to advancing AI-powered autonomous public safety and emergency communication technologies in its mission to enhance national safety and security efforts.
news.headline: Knightscope expands with new 33,355 sq ft Silicon Valley HQ at 305 N Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, marking 12th anniversary.

news.summary: Sense is expanding into the Japanese market through a new partnership to innovate smart meters with data capabilities of up to 1MHz. The goal is to enhance grid efficiency, safety, and reliability. Based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, the collaboration between Sense and Chugoku Keiki signifies a significant step towards modernizing energy infrastructure. The partnership aims to leverage advanced technology to shape a more sustainable and connected grid.
news.headline: Sense partners with CHK for Japan entry, developing smart meters with up to 1MHz data for grid efficiency.
Trump: Inflation is down.

news.summary: TimelyCare, a trusted virtual health and well-being provider in higher education, has introduced a new feature allowing instant phone connection with their providers without the need for logging in or using an app. This aims to enhance access to critical mental health support. The service was launched in Fort Worth, Texas on April 10, 2025.
news.headline: TimelyCare introduces 24/7 crisis line for instant mental health support without login or app. Based in Fort Worth, Texas.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in a particular region. The local health authorities confirmed a total of 500 new cases in the past week, bringing the total number of confirmed cases in the area to 2,000. These new cases have raised concerns among officials about the potential for a surge in infections. The authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines, including wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and washing hands frequently. Additionally, efforts are being made to enhance testing and contact tracing to better control the spread of the virus in the community. The situation is being closely monitored, and further updates will be provided as the situation evolves.
news.headline: Borregaard ASA issues commercial papers.

news.summary: The ITS Logistics US Port/Rail Ramp Freight Index forecast for April 2025 indicates continued frontloading through March, with strong import volumes expected in April. This trend is due to containers with frontloaded inventory reaching the U.S. by April 9th. Import and export volumes are expected to experience a "cliff event" in May and possibly into June, similar to the impacts felt during the immediate COVID response. This event will occur as excess frontloaded inventory is utilized and shippers delay sourcing efforts to minimize tariff cost impacts. Trucking capacity, container storage, terminal congestion, and chassis availability are expected to tighten as frontloaded inventory is absorbed into U.S. supply chains. Ports and ocean carriers not servicing export shippers are facing empty termination issues. CNBC reported a steep decline in new freight order activity across the U.S. and a quarterly decline in Chinese imports due to the frontloading trend.
news.headline: ITS Logistics predicts a potential drop in import and export volumes in the U.S. due to frontloading. Trucking capacity and terminal congestion expected to tighten as frontloaded inventory is absorbed. Cliff event similar to COVID response anticipated through May and June.

news.summary: In Dublin, Ohio, Quantum Health, a prominent consumer healthcare navigation and care coordination organization, received a Silver Stevie® Award for Front-Line Customer Service Team of the Year at the 19th annual Stevie Awards for Sales & Customer Service. This esteemed international accolade highlights outstanding performance in key business areas. Quantum Health gratefully acknowledges this recognition of its customer service team, known as He.
news.headline: Quantum Health's service team awarded Silver Stevie® for Customer Service Team of the Year at 19th Stevie Awards.

news.summary: The BCC Research study predicts that the Global DNA Sequencing market will grow from $14.8 billion in 2024 to $34.8 billion by 2029, with a CAGR of 18.6% during the forecast period. The report delves into current and potential market trends, covering technologies like NGS, TGS, pyrosequencing, and Sanger sequencing. Segmented by product, end user, application, and disease category, the report also profiles major companies and analyzes their business segments. Geographically, North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Rest of the World are covered, focusing on personalized medicine's increasing demand. Factors driving market growth include technological advances, clinical diagnostics, precision medicine, and noninvasive prenatal testing.	getline_markupThe full article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: DNA Sequencing industry to reach $34.8B by 2029, growing from $14.8B in 2024 at 18.6% CAGR. Focus on NGS, TGS, pyrosequencing, and Sanger sequencing. Market exploring personalized medicine, oncology, rare disease diagnosis, and prenatal testing. Geographical breakdown in North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Rest of the World. Major companies and technological advances driving growth, includes clinical diagnostics, precision medicine, and NIPT. Full story on Benzinga.com.
